zh.lifeSummary: Lady Joanna Alwyn Gaylord Brixton於1509年出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特比特明斯特。她於1525年與Newman John Brixton of SEVENOAK IV在英國英格蘭薩默塞特結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1574年在她的家鄉享年66歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Joanna Alwyn Gaylord Brixton于1509年出生在英国英格兰薩默塞特比特明斯特。她于1525年与Newman John Brixton of SEVENOAK IV在英国英格兰薩默塞特结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1574年在她的家乡享年66岁。

nameMeanings.name: Gaylord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern, of Norman origin): variant of Gaylard altered by folk etymology, from the Old French and Middle English personal name Gaillart, Gallard, Galliard, representing either the ancient Germanic personal name Gailhard (composed of elements meaning ‘powerful, lusty, cheerful’ + ‘hard, firm, resilient, strong’) or Old French gaillard, gaillart ‘vigorous, powerful, strong’. Alternatively, from Middle English gaillard, galiard ‘brave, strong, valiant’, also ‘lively, gay, gaily dressed’.
Altered form of Gaylard, itself an Anglicized form of French Gaillard , which was brought to England by the Huguenots.
In some cases also an Americanized form of French   Gaillard  .

variants.name: Gaillard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Philippe, Pierre, Andre, Andree, Ketly, Marcel, Mirlande, Monique.
French: nickname from gaillard ‘vigorous, strong’ (ultimately from Celtic galia ‘strength’). This surname (in any of the two possible senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Gailhard, composed of the elements gail ‘powerful, lusty, cheerful’ + hard ‘hard, strong’.

nameMeanings.name: Brixton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name perhaps from a lost or unidentified place, although possibly brought to Scotland from one or more of the English Brintons under 2 below.

English: habitational name from Brinton in Norfolk, named in Old English as Brȳningtūn ‘settlement (Old English tūn) associated with (-ing-) Brȳni’ (a personal name based on Old English bryne ‘fire, flame’), or from any of various other places with names of the same origin, such as Brineton in Staffordshire, Great Brington in Essex, Brimpton in Berkshire, Brimington in Derbyshire, Brenton in Devon, Brington in Cambridgeshire or (Great and Little) Brington in Northamptonshire.
History: William Brinton (1635–99) came from Staffordshire, England, to West Chester, PA, in 1684–85.

variants.name: Garnto
meanings: Presumably an altered form of English Brinton , via the intermediate forms Brinto and Grinto. Compare Gornto .

variants.name: Gornto
meanings: Presumably an altered form of English Brinton , via the intermediate forms Brinto and Grinto. Compare Garnto .
History: Edward Brinto, alias Gornto, is listed as one of the original settlers (a mason) in Jamestown VA in 1607.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Joanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

variants.name: Joannah
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). variants.name: Joana
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. nameMeanings.name: Alwyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ælf ‘elf, supernatural being’ + wine ‘friend’. This was not especially common in Britain either before or after the Norman Conquest, but it is popular in the United States. The reasons for this are not entirely clear; association with Calvin may be a factor. A more plausible (though less elevated) explanation is that this was the name given to the naughty chipmunk in a popular television cartoon series of the 1960s.

variants.name: Aylwin
meanings: Variant of   Alvin .

en.lifeSummary: Lady Joanna Alwyn Gaylord Brixton was born in 1509, in Pitminster, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. She married Newman John Brixton of SEVENOAK IV in 1525, in Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died about 1574, in her hometown, at the age of 66.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Joanna Alwyn Gaylord Brixton est née en 1509, à Pitminster, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Newman John Brixton of SEVENOAK IV en 1525, à Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1574, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 66 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Joanna Alwyn Gaylord Brixton wurde 1509 in Pitminster, Somerset, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat 1525 in Somerset, England, United Kingdom Newman John Brixton of SEVENOAK IV geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1574 im Alter von 66 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Joanna Alwyn Gaylord Brixton è nata il nel 1509 nel Pitminster, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei sposato Newman John Brixton of SEVENOAK IV nel 1525, nel Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì circa 1574, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 66 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Joanna Alwyn Gaylord Brixton(은)는 1509년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 피트민스터에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1525년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트에서 Newman John Brixton of SEVENOAK IV(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1574년 그녀의 고향에서 66세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Joanna Alwyn Gaylord Brixton родилась в 1509 г. в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Newman John Brixton of SEVENOAK IV в 1525 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла около 1574 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 66 лет.
